summary: Clean, idiomatic implementation that faithfully realizes tech-spec Decisions 2/3/5/6/8 and the Data Models column layouts. Count screen emits the exact 16-column order with DiffIntvl [4,14]; time screen emits 10 columns with [4,8]; every diffed column (and every NULL-able KiB byte source) is coalesce-guarded; KiB uses integer /1024 with bigint cast on PG18; synthetic io_key is built via left(md5(...),10) with object/context coalesced inside md5; both screens share the identical count-based WHERE. Naming, comment style, and selector signatures match the reference files. No critical, major, or tech-debt findings; only minor observations.

findings.description: The PG16/17 branch is reached via an implicit else (return after the `if version >= PostgresV18` block) rather than an explicit `if version >= PostgresV16` guard. Decision 8 phrases the contract as 'version >= PostgresV18 / >= PostgresV16'. This is functionally correct and intentional (the task body explicitly says versions < PG16 must still return the PG16 form because the MinRequiredVersion gate lives at the view layer and Format must stay safe), and it mirrors SelectStatBgwriterQuery's trailing fallthrough. PostgresV15/16/17 are therefore declared in this task but consumed by later waves (view map MinRequiredVersion), not by io.go.
findings.suggestion: No change required. The fallthrough-to-PG16 form is correct and documented; leaving an unconditional final return matches the bgwriter reference and keeps the selector Format-safe for PG14/15. If desired purely for symmetry with the Decision 8 wording, a clarifying inline comment on the final return already exists in the function doc-comment — sufficient.

findings.description: The count-based WHERE clause (the long coalesce-sum predicate) is duplicated verbatim across all three SQL constants (PgStatIOPG16, PgStatIOPG18, PgStatIOTime). Decision 5 mandates the WHERE be identical on every screen so the row-sets match, so the duplication is semantically required and correct — but it is hand-copied in three places, which is a latent drift risk if a counter is ever added/renamed.
findings.suggestion: Acceptable as-is given the reference files also inline their SQL and pgcenter does not compose SQL from fragments. Optional: extract the predicate into an unexported `const pgStatIOCountWhere = "WHERE coalesce(reads,0)+..."` and concatenate it into all three constants, guaranteeing the three screens can never diverge. Low priority; the NULL-safety test already pins the coalesced columns, and a row-set-parity assertion could be added in a later wave if drift is a concern.

findings.description: SelectStatIOTimeQuery ignores its version parameter (named `_`). This is correct and intentional — the timing column set is schema-stable across PG16/17/18, matching the SelectStatReplicationSlotsQuery precedent — and the doc-comment explains it well. Noting only that a future PG version changing a *_time column would require revisiting this single-branch assumption.
findings.suggestion: No change required. The `_` parameter and the explanatory comment match the replslots reference idiom exactly. The assumption is sound for PG16-18; revisit only if a future PG release alters the *_time schema.

spec_conformance_checks.count_ncols_16_diffintvl_4_14: pass — SelectStatIOQuery returns (query, 16, [2]int{4,14}) on all branches; verified by Test_SelectStatIOQuery over {14,15,16,17,18}
spec_conformance_checks.time_ncols_10_diffintvl_4_8: pass — SelectStatIOTimeQuery returns (query, 10, [2]int{4,8}); verified by Test_SelectStatIOTimeQuery
spec_conformance_checks.column_order_count: pass — io_key,backend_type,object,context,reads,read,KiB,writes,write,KiB,extends,ext,KiB,hits,evictions,writebacks,reuses,fsyncs,stats_age matches Data Models exactly (KiB interleaved at idx 5/7/9)
spec_conformance_checks.coalesce_on_every_diffed_column: pass — all 11 count counters and both KiB byte-sources (op_bytes PG16/17; read_bytes/write_bytes/extend_bytes PG18) and all 5 time columns wrapped in coalesce(...,0); asserted by NullSafety tests
spec_conformance_checks.kib_integer_division: pass — PG16/17: coalesce(reads,0)*coalesce(op_bytes,0)/1024 (integer); PG18: (coalesce(read_bytes,0)/1024)::bigint, cast prevents numeric decimals per Decision 6
spec_conformance_checks.op_bytes_branch_vs_native_bytes: pass — version>=PostgresV18 -> native *_bytes; else op_bytes multiplier per Decision 3
spec_conformance_checks.synthetic_io_key: pass — left(md5(backend_type||coalesce(object,'')||coalesce(context,'')),10) AS io_key; object/context coalesced inside md5 so a NULL dimension cannot yield a NULL key that would break diff() row matching on column 0
spec_conformance_checks.count_based_where_on_both_screens: pass — identical coalesce-sum WHERE on PgStatIOPG16, PgStatIOPG18, PgStatIOTime per Decision 5
spec_conformance_checks.version_constants: pass — PostgresV15/16/17/18 added to query.go const block after PostgresV14; PostgresV18 referenced in selector branches per Decision 8
spec_conformance_checks.format_safety: pass — flat non-template SQL; Test_SelectStatIOQuery_NoTemplateArtifacts guards against stray {{ that would break query.Format
spec_conformance_checks.diff_machinery_compatibility: pass — coalesce guarantees no empty strings reaching parsePairInt; integer KiB output (no dots) routes through parsePairInt not parsePairFloat; UniqueKey column 0 and stats_age sit outside DiffIntvl
